definition: Rate differentials explain 8-12% of FX variance in low-vol regimes vs. 0-2% in high-vol regimes. The regime-switching structure is confirmed by a Chow test (F=7.2 on ~15,000 pair-months) and is one of only 3-4 claims surviving HLZ-adjusted multiple-testing thresholds (t≥3.8).
evidence: Chow test on R² regime-switching significant at F=7.2 on ~15,000 pair-months
Survived tautology correction with t-stat reduced from 3.87 to ~2.0-2.5
Robust to both implied and realized vol conditioning specifications
open_questions: Transitional dynamics between regimes less well-characterized
Describes equilibrium not causal mechanism — limited predictive utility
Whether the relationship holds out-of-sample in EM pairs
